title: Docker -v broad host bind (-v /etc:/data:ro) → BLOCK (ADR 0012 -v branch)

origin.description: Hand-written contract seed locking the docker -v branch of the ADR 0012 host-path extractor at the corpus gate. C023 locks the --mount form; this locks the colon-separated -v form. The host side /etc is a broad root; the :ro option and the container dst /data must be ignored. Chosen as a high-value lock because /etc:/data is NOT caught by the plain-arg loop (it is '/etc:' not '/etc/'), so only the docker -v extractor + dockerVolumeHostSide split can flag it.

humanExpectation.rationale: The docker -v argument '/etc:/data:ro' binds the host's /etc into the container. The ADR 0012 host-path extractor splits the host side (/etc) from the container dst (/data) and the :ro option, and /etc is a broad root → files.broad-path (critical blocker) → BLOCK. The plain-arg loop alone would MISS this ('/etc:/data:ro' neither equals nor startsWith '/etc/'), so this case proves the -v extractor is doing the work. thisCaseMustNeverBeSafe: true.
knownLimitations: Synthetic: a minimal, unambiguous -v broad-bind shape. The evidence value is the extracted host path /etc, never the container dst /data.
